title: CEO of The Atlantic encourages “engaged optimism” about AI in WashU talk
summary_en: Nicholas Thompson, former Editor-in-Chief of WIRED and now CEO of The Atlantic, has reported on artificial intelligence since 2017. As part of WashU’s +AI Perspectives Week, Thompson reflected on what he’s learned about AI and his advice for college students to a packed crowd of WashU students and community members on March 25.
author: Zach Trabitz, Riley Herron
title_cn: [待翻译] CEO of The Atlantic encourages “engaged optimism” about AI in WashU talk

title: ‘Why now?’: Professor Krister Knapp discusses the nuance of conflict in Iran
summary_en: Professor Krister Knapp spoke on the ongoing war in Iran, assessing and explaining both the origins and possible outcomes of the conflict, March 19.
author: Zoe Rhodes
title_cn: [待翻译] ‘Why now?’: Professor Krister Knapp discusses the nuance of conflict in Iran

title: Spring 2026 Student Union election names new leadership and denies EST block funding
summary_en: Student Union (SU) announced the results of its spring 2026 elections via Instagram on March 26, naming a new executive board and legislative body following a multi-day voting period that began Tuesday at 9 p.m. CST and concluded Thursday at 9 p.m. CST.
author: Victor Huang
title_cn: [待翻译] Spring 2026 Student Union election names new leadership and denies EST block funding
